expectations.text: Review references 'v18/dev' as the target branch (not 'main')
expectations.evidence: The review header states: 'PR: #22217, Target: origin/v18/dev'.

expectations.text: Review discusses the behavioral impact of changing the default payload type
expectations.evidence: The review summary states: 'Changes the default webhook payload type from WebhookPayloadType.Legacy to WebhookPayloadType.Minimal in Constants.Webhooks.DefaultPayloadType.' And the correctness section mentions the remark is stale since 'this PR makes Minimal the actual default for v18'. However, the behavioral impact on users (they will silently receive different payloads) is not explicitly discussed.
The review is 83 lines, under the 150-line threshold.

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues (formatting, whitespace, naming conventions, comment grammar, code style preferences) unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue -- it is a legitimate finding.
expectations.evidence: No cosmetic/stylistic findings. All findings are about documentation accuracy and code quality.

expectations.text: Does NOT flag the ~30 builder extension methods with Legacy defaults (pre-existing, not changed in the PR)
expectations.evidence: No mention of builder extension methods or Legacy defaults in methods outside the PR scope.

expectations.text: Flags stale WebhookSettings.cs docs as a side-effect of the constant value change
expectations.evidence: The review does NOT mention WebhookSettings.cs at all. It flags the stale remark on WebhookPayloadType.Minimal (line 12) but does not identify the stale documentation in WebhookSettings.cs which still references Legacy as the default.

expectations.text: Identifies affected consumers outside the PR (WebhookSettings, UmbracoBuilder, or WebhookEventCollectionBuilderExtensions)
expectations.evidence: The review does not trace the DefaultPayloadType constant to its downstream consumers. It does not mention WebhookSettings, UmbracoBuilder.Collections, or WebhookEventCollectionBuilderExtensions.

eval_feedback.overall: Concise and focused review but misses side-effect detection -- does not trace the constant change to WebhookSettings.cs stale docs or identify downstream consumers.
